message:
franken_numpy-k3r fix dtype name aliasing, cross product 2D support, busday NaT handling, batch_solve caller-driven vector_rhs

DType naming and parsing (fnp-dtype):
- Rename DType::I8::name() from "i8" to "int8" and DType::U8::name() from "u8"
  to "uint8" to avoid collision with NumPy's byte-width shorthand convention
  where "i8" means 8-byte int (int64) and "u8" means 8-byte uint (uint64).
- Remap parse() so "i8" -> I64 and "u8" -> U64 (byte-width convention), while
  removing the old "i8_" and "u8_" workaround aliases.
- Add parse_numpy_byte_width_shorthand test validating all iN/uN/fN byte-width
  aliases resolve correctly.
- Handle negative zero in min_scalar_type: return F16 for -0.0 instead of
  misclassifying it as boolean/integer zero.

Conformance fixtures (fnp-conformance):
- Update dtype_differential_cases.json to use "int8"/"uint8" canonical names
  in can_cast test case rhs fields, matching the new DType::name() output.
- Pass vector_rhs flag to batch_solve call in execute_linalg_operation, derived
  from comparing rhs_shape dimensionality against lhs shape.

Linear algebra (fnp-linalg):
- Change batch_solve signature to accept an explicit `vector_rhs: bool` parameter
  instead of inferring RHS interpretation from shape dimensionality heuristics.
  The caller (fnp-conformance) now decides whether the RHS is a vector or matrix,
  eliminating ambiguous cases where shapes could be interpreted either way.
- Fix eigvalsh_nxn doc comment: eigenvalues are returned in ascending order
  (matching NumPy convention), not descending as previously documented.
- Fix eigh_nxn doc comment: eigenvectors are stored as columns in a row-major
  n*n array, not column-major as previously documented.
- Fix near-singular back-substitution in eig_nxn_full: use sign-preserving
  epsilon (1e-15 with original sign of denom) to prevent eigenvector sign flips
  when the denominator is very small but negative.
- Add test_batch_solve_broadcasting_bug integration test for batched identity
  matrix solve with broadcasting.

Ufunc operations (fnp-ufunc):
- Rewrite cross() to support 2-D and 3-D vectors as well as mixed 2D/3D inputs,
  matching NumPy's np.cross semantics: 2D x 2D returns scalar, 2D x 3D or
  3D x 3D returns 3-element vector. Previous implementation only supported 3-D.
- Add tests: cross_3d, cross_2d_scalar, cross_mixed_2d_3d, update cross_wrong_length
  to cross_mixed_length verifying the new mixed-dimension behavior.
- Fix infer_dtype_from_values to handle negative zero: -0.0 should not classify
  as boolean (it is not the same as +0.0 for IEEE 754 purposes) and should not
  classify as integer.
- Fix parse_fixed_signature_dtype to use canonical long-form dtype names
  (int8, uint8, int16, etc.) instead of short aliases, ensuring consistency
  with the updated DType::parse() mapping.
- Add NaT (NaN) handling to is_busday: return 0.0 (false) for NaT inputs
  instead of producing undefined behavior from casting NaN to i64.
- Add NaT error propagation to busday_count: return an explicit error message
  when either start or end date is NaT, matching NumPy's behavior.
- Add NaT error propagation to busday_offset: return errors for NaT date inputs
  and NaN offset inputs. Previously, weekend dates with offset=0 returned NaN
  and checked post-hoc; now all error paths return Result::Err immediately.

Feature parity (FEATURE_PARITY.md):
- Update test counts: fnp-ufunc 1372->1406, fnp-linalg 199->200,
  fnp-random 183->184, fnp-iter 102->103, fnp-io 146->149,
  fnp-dtype 116->117. Total 2358->2397.
- Add "NumPy byte-width parsing" to fnp-dtype description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 ## Remaining Gaps (Python-specific, low priority)
